headline: The agent tooling stack cooled this quarter as distributed training techniques broke out across the field.
body: The established agent and orchestration layer weakened across the board, with prompt engineering, orchestration, AI layer, and agent framework all losing momentum even at high attention ratios, while AI context management, scoped skills, and Model Context Protocol lagged further. The breakout activity rotated toward systems-level training and serving methods: DeepSpeed Ulysses, Untied Ulysses, CPU offloading, and auto-scaling all entered as new outliers, alongside attribution, collision detection, percolation, and filter propagation. On the product side, persistent context and memory work continues through PAI built on Claude Code [0], and open model deployment advanced with Gemma 4's 31B model placing fourth on the LM Arena open leaderboard while running on a single GPU and on device [1]. The legal sanctions over AI-generated fake case citations [11] underscored ongoing hallucination risk in high-stakes use.

cited_items.title: PAI: The Life OS for AI-Powered Development
Sovereign Escape Velocity: Ownership w Open Models — Gus Martins, & Ian Ballantyne, Google DeepMind
Lawyers Barred for A.I.-Generated Citations to Fake Cases - The New York Times
